title: Persistent facial pain with sequential antibiotic failure and PIFP diagnosis
description: An 11-year-old boy presents with persistent right cheek pain following a streptococcal pharyngitis episode. Sequential antibiotic courses (amoxicillin, cefditoren pivoxil, clarithromycin) fail to resolve the pain, and classical trigeminal neuralgia is excluded by MRI. The final diagnosis is persistent idiopathic facial pain (PIFP) with a somatoform component, managed with carbamazepine (not tolerated) and psychological counseling. Data sourced from AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes dataset (idx: 203500).
notes: From AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes dataset (idx: 203500). The synthetic conversation covers only the initial clinical encounter up to the clarithromycin prescription; the full_note contains the complete longitudinal course including PIFP diagnosis, carbamazepine trial, and psychotherapy outcome. Carbamazepine is included in medication_list despite being ceased after one dose, as it is clinically significant and creates a notable drug interaction signal with clarithromycin (CYP3A4 inhibition). RxNorm IDs for all four medications require verification against the NIH RxNav API before running the benchmark evaluator.
